items.title: PHASE 26 // THERMAL ENVELOPE
items.subtitle: Sustained load capture — silicon held inside spec

thread.hook: Phase 26 thermal envelope was captured across a six hour sustained load window.
body: Fourteen zones were sampled at two hertz on an eight megahertz telemetry bus. Package temperature peaked at 71.4C against a 63.1C mean.
VRM topped out at 78.0C. NVMe held under 55C. The fan curve drifted four percent and was recalibrated mid-window.
Zero throttle events were logged. The envelope was held end to end.
thread.closer: The numbers were recorded, not promised. Phase 26 ran cold.

lines.text: quote signed by tpm — chain intact

thread.hook: A cold start boot primitive was traced and every stage was attested.
body: Stage zero ROM, stage one loader, and stage two kernel were each measured into separate PCR banks.
The TPM quote was signed and the chain held intact. Cold start latency was recorded at 1.83 seconds.
The remote verifier accepted the attestation without exception.
thread.closer: Trust was measured at every hop. Nothing was assumed.

metrics.thesis: Centralized custody concentrated the blast radius. Sovereign custody distributed it.

thread.hook: The JMIR 2024 ransomware survey was reviewed against a sovereign custody posture.
body: Three hundred seventy four breaches were reported in the study window. Eighty eight point seven million records were exposed.
Median downtime was logged at twenty one days. Mean incident cost reached 10.9 million dollars per event.
Each figure traced back to one structural cause — custody was centralized, so the blast radius was concentrated.
thread.closer: Sovereign custody distributed the blast radius. The data was peer reviewed, not marketed.

metrics.thesis: Recovery latency was a property of architecture, not of incident response effort.

thread.hook: Restore latency was mapped directly onto the custody model in the JMIR supplement.
body: Centralized estates logged a twenty one day median restore. Sovereign estates logged 4.2 hours.
The delta factor reached roughly one hundred twenty times. Ransom paid under the sovereign model was zero.
The gap was not effort. The gap was structure — backups were held where the operator held the keys.
thread.closer: Recovery latency was a property of architecture. The architecture was the control.

items.title: SOVEREIGN NODE // REFERENCE BUILD
items.subtitle: Prosumer hardware mapped to a hardened stack

thread.hook: A sovereign node reference build was drafted for prosumer hardware.
body: The chassis was specified as a 4U twelve-bay frame around a sixteen core ECC-capable platform with 128GB of ECC DDR5.
Storage was laid out as an NVMe mirror over an eight-disk RAID-Z2 array. The root volume sat behind measured boot and LUKS2.
Ingress was reduced to WireGuard with no public 443. Keys were held on-node and TPM sealed. Backups followed 3-2-1 with an offline copy.
thread.closer: Every block was a decision the operator owned. The blueprint was published, not gatekept.

thread.hook: An egress hardening template was mapped for self-hosted estates.
body: Outbound policy was set to default-deny. DNS was pinned to a resolver over DoT and time was pulled from an internal stratum-1 source.
Updates were restricted to a signed mirror pull. Logs were shipped append-only to an off-node sink.
Workloads were sorted into tiers — air-gapped, allowlist-only, and metered — each measured weekly against a baseline diff.
thread.closer: The map turned outbound traffic into an explicit decision. Silence was the default.
